state.next-action:
Session 2026-04-11 — eval framework evolution batch completed.
=== COMPLETED ===
| # | Type | Task | Status | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | Framework | domain + tech_stack + build_requirement + human_estimate in gad.json | done | | 2 | CLI | gad eval inherit-skills (copy emergent skills between runs) | done | | 3 | Eval | Categorized all 26 eval projects by domain | done | | 4 | Eval | Scaffolded 4 tech-stack evals: Phaser, PixiJS, Three.js, Babylon.js | done | | 5 | Pipeline | Per-phase pressure scores in self-eval (10 high-pressure phases found) | done | | 6 | Site | GanttChart component (click to expand, hover for stats) | done | | 7 | Docs | quick-start.md + eval-guide.md | done | | 8 | CLI | gad eval readme (inject scores into project README) | done |
=== DECISIONS (gad-110 through gad-119) === 110: Eval domain categories (game/skill/software/business/stories/tooling) 111: Tech stack comparison evals 112: gad eval inherit-skills CLI 113: Eval timestamps + build requirements + completion gates 114: Human time estimation vs agent time comparison 115: Pressure per phase drives skill creation opportunities 116: Playable archive redesign for thousands of evals (queued Phase 28+) 117: Quick start guide — no repo clone required 118: GAD discipline scoring for framework evals 119: Interactive Gantt chart for phases and rounds
=== PRESSURE DATA (top 5 phases) === Phase 22: 110 (58 tasks, 26 crosscuts) — HIGHEST Phase 21: 56 (28 tasks, 14 crosscuts) Phase 14.5: 21 (7 tasks, 7 crosscuts) Phase 20: 20 (8 tasks, 6 crosscuts) Phase 14: 19 (7 tasks, 6 crosscuts)
=== NEW CLI SURFACE === gad eval inherit-skills --from project/vN --to project gad eval readme --project name gad eval list now shows DOMAIN column TRACE.json gains: domain, tech_stack, build_requirement, human_estimate_hours, source_size_bytes, build_size_bytes
=== EVAL PROJECTS: 30 total (was 26) === 4 new: etd-phaser, etd-pixijs, etd-threejs, etd-babylonjs
=== NEXT SESSION === 1. Wire GanttChart into Workflow + RoundResults sections (user refactoring landing page) 2. Wire SelfEval component into page 3. Run round 5 skills testing: GAD+Skills with/without inheritance 4. Run tech-stack comparison evals 5. First reverse-engineer eval on grime-time 6. Playable archive redesign for multi-domain + thousands of evals (Phase 28) 7. User plays round 5 builds and scores them
